message: Command `librelane --help 2>&1 | tail -60` executed with exit code 0.
content:
Sequential flow controls:
  -F, --from TEXT                Start from a step with this id. Supported by
                                 sequential flows.
  -T, --to TEXT                  Stop at a step with this id. Supported by
                                 sequential flows.
  --only TEXT                    Shorthand to set both --from and --to to the
                                 same value. Overrides the values from both.
  -S, --skip TEXT                Skip these steps. Supported by sequential
                                 flows.

Run options: [mutually exclusive]
  --run-tag TEXT                 An optional name to use for this particular
                                 run of an LibreLane-based flow. Used to create
                                 the run directory.
  --last-run                     Use the last run as the run tag.

Flow configuration options:
  -f, --flow [optimizing|classic|vhdlclassic|chip|openinklayout|openinopenroad|openinmagic|synthesisexploration]
                                 The built-in LibreLane flow to use for this
                                 run
  -c, --override-config TEXT     For this run only- override a configuration
                                 variable with a certain value. In the format
                                 KEY=VALUE. Can be specified multiple times.
                                 Values must be valid JSON values, and keys
                                 must not use their deprecated names.

Other options:
  -e, --initial-state-element-override TEXT
                                 Elements to override in the used initial state
                                 in the format DESIGN_FORMAT_ID=PATH
  -j, --jobs INTEGER             The maximum number of threads or processes
                                 that can be used by LibreLane.  [default: 8]
  --condensed / --full           In condensed mode, subprocess logs are
                                 suppressed regardless of step, --hide-
                                 progress-bar is the default, and the log
                                 messages themselves are a bit more terse.
                                 Useful for debugging.  [default: full]
  --show-progress-bar / --hide-progress-bar
                                 Whether to show the progress bar when running
                                 Flows. [default: show]
  --log-level [ALL or 0|DEBUG or 10|SUBPROCESS or 12|VERBOSE or 15|INFO or 20|WARNING or 30|ERROR or 40|CRITICAL or 50]
                                 A logging level. Set to VERBOSE or higher to
                                 silence subprocess logs. [default: unchanged
                                 from SUBPROCESS]
  --reproducible TEXT            Create a reproducible for the step matching
                                 this ID, then abort the flow. Supported by
                                 sequential flows.
  --overwrite                    Overwrite run, if exists.
  --design-dir DIRECTORY         The top-level directory for your design that
                                 configuration objects may resolve paths
                                 relative to.
  -i, --with-initial-state FILE  Use these JSON files as an initial state. If
                                 multiple are provided, they are merged with
                                 keys in later files overriding keys in prior
                                 files. If none are specified, the latest
                                 `state_out.json` of the run directory will be
                                 used. If none exist, an empty initial state is
                                 created.
  --help                         Show this message and exit.
